title: Is abortion permissible for a pregnancy that occurred outside of marriage?
summary: Abortion before 120 days (4 months) is permissible in cases of pregnancy outside of marriage, as the ruh (soul) has not yet been blown in. After 120 days, it is generally not allowed.

title: Are stock options halal in Islam?
summary: Stock options are haram (impermissible) according to the vast majority of Islamic finance bodies including the OIC Fiqh Academy, European Fatwa Council, and AAOIFI, due to excessive gharar (uncertainty) and qimar (gambling).

title: Is Bitcoin and cryptocurrency halal or haram in Islam?
summary: Yasir Qadhi holds that Bitcoin and established cryptocurrencies are permissible (halal) based on the default Islamic legal principle that all transactions are permissible unless proven otherwise. He a

title: What are the Islamic rulings regarding a child born out of wedlock?
summary: Yasir Qadhi provides a comprehensive ruling on children born out of wedlock: the child is absolutely blameless and deserves full love, dignity, and respect regardless of the circumstances of their bir
tags: does illegitimate child inherit from father

title: Does a civil divorce filed by the wife count as an Islamic divorce?
summary: When a husband files for civil divorce and signs the paperwork, it counts as an Islamic talaq because writing a divorce is the same as verbalizing it. However, when a wife files for civil divorce, the
tags: does civil divorce count as Islamic divorce

title: Is taking a conventional mortgage from a bank considered the prohibited riba?
summary: The riba in modern mortgages is a secondary type prohibited by hadith, not the primary Quranic riba. Major fatwa councils permit conventional mortgages under conditions of genuine need such as buying a home.

title: Is cosmetic surgery permissible to correct a birth defect or for beautification?
summary: Corrective surgery to fix a birth defect, acquired disfigurement, or abnormality is permissible by near-unanimous scholarly consensus, based on the Islamic maxim that harm should be eliminated. Howeve
tags: is cosmetic surgery halal in islam

title: To what extent can a divorced woman advertise that she is looking for a husband?
summary: It is permissible for a divorced woman to make it known in a dignified manner that she is seeking a husband, including through word of mouth and Islamic marriage websites.

title: Is it permissible to drink water while standing?
summary: It is better to sit when drinking water, but drinking while standing is permissible. Ibn Abbas reported seeing the Prophet drink while standing. When circumstances make standing natural, there is no issue.

title: Is saying "Oh my God" in frustration to parents equivalent to the prohibited "uff"?
summary: The prohibition of saying "uff" is about expressing frustration and contempt toward parents, not the specific word. Any expression said with a tone of frustration or contempt carries the same prohibition.

title: What should you do about halal meat standards when visiting someone else's home?
summary: If you follow a stricter halal meat opinion, you may politely ask about your host's standards without offending them. Participate in the gathering and eat vegetarian if needed. Stunning is makruh but does not make the animal haram.

title: Does haram money become halal when inherited?
summary: By default, inherited money does not require source investigation. If wealth was clearly and specifically stolen from identifiable people, it must be returned; for generically haram earnings with no specific claimant, the inheritor may keep it though giving sadaqah is recommended.
tags: does haram money become halal through inheritance
can I keep inheritance from haram income
islamic ruling on inheriting interest money

title: Is house, car, and life insurance permitted in Islam?
summary: Car and health insurance are permitted due to necessity in Western countries. House and property insurance are also allowed. Life insurance remains impermissible unless provided automatically by an employer.

title: Can Muslims invest in stocks and the S&P 500?
summary: Investing in stocks is permissible in Islam if certain conditions are met. The company's primary product must be halal, and the proportion of interest-based loans and liquid assets must fall within ac
tags: is stock trading halal or haram

title: Is missing salah really worse than murder in Islam?
summary: The statement that missing salah is "worse than murder" is not a direct Quranic or Prophetic phrase but a derived framing from a minority Hanbali view on total abandonment of prayer. Yasir Qadhi stresses the majority disagreement and distinguishes sin gravity from forgiveness, noting sins against people remain harder to clear.
tags: does missing salah make you kafir
sin of missing prayer vs murder

title: Is it appropriate for a husband to joke about taking a second wife?
summary: The Prophet (peace be upon him) never joked about taking a second wife, and doing so is not from the Sunnah. A woman has feelings, and it is not gentlemanly for a husband to provoke those feelings by

title: Is there any leeway in keeping a dog as a pet in Islam?
summary: Dogs are unanimously allowed for valid benefits such as hunting, guarding, and service needs, while keeping them purely as pets is disallowed by the majority but treated more leniently in a Maliki strand. Yasir Qadhi personally treats dog saliva as najis while affirming kindness to dogs and acknowledging legitimate scholarly differences.
tags: is it haram to have a dog
angels not entering house with dog

title: Can a wife leave the house and go to her parents during the iddah period after talaq?
summary: After a husband gives one talaq, the wife should remain in the same house for the three-month iddah period (three menstrual cycles) as a safeguard Allah has placed to protect the marriage. Leaving the
tags: can wife leave house after talaq
where should wife stay during iddah

title: Is it permissible to remove a family member from life support or a ventilator?
summary: Medical treatment including ventilators is mubah (permissible) but not wajib (obligatory). Removing life support from someone in a vegetative state with no chance of recovery is not considered murder.

title: How do I make up for years of missed salah after becoming practicing?
summary: There are two overlapping scholarly opinions on making up years of missed salah. The majority opinion holds that one should make up the missed prayers (qada) by praying extra over the same number of y
tags: how to make up missed prayers in Islam
do I have to make up years of missed salah
can you make up missed prayers in Islam

title: Is it allowed for a husband to force his wife into intimacy in Islam?
summary: A husband does not have the right to force himself upon his wife in Islam. While earlier scholars may have allowed this based on their cultural context and ijtihad, this opinion is not binding because
tags: can a husband force his wife in Islam

title: Does Islamic law dictate specific household chores and roles between husband and wife?
summary: Islamic law does not dictate specific chore assignments. The Quranic principle is ma'ruf (treating each other with kindness normal for their time and culture), with arbitration and divorce as escalating steps for disputes.

title: How does a married daughter balance obligations between her husband and her parents?
summary: A married woman's primary obligation shifts to her husband, but her relationship with parents never ceases. The husband must be fair and give her reasonable opportunity to care for her parents.

title: Can you wipe over regular (non-leather) socks during wudu?
summary: It is permissible to wipe over regular cloth socks during wudu; they do not need to be leather. The conditions are that socks must cover the ankles and you must have had wudu before putting them on.

title: Can you break your salah for a medical emergency in the masjid?
summary: It is not only permissible but obligatory to break one's salah to attend to a medical emergency. If someone faints or has a medical emergency during congregational prayer, the one or two people neares
tags: can you break prayer for emergency
someone faints in masjid during salah
is it allowed to break salah

title: Is wearing a necklace, earrings, or jewelry permissible for men in Islam?
summary: Gold and silk are categorically forbidden for men by explicit prophetic prohibition — this applies to gold chains, rings, necklaces, and any silk clothing regardless of whether one is alone or in publ
tags: can men wear necklaces in islam
is it haram for men to wear earrings
men wearing chains halal or haram
islamic ruling on men wearing jewelry
can muslim men wear silver chains

title: Is leaving a negative review for a Muslim-owned restaurant considered backbiting?
summary: A negative factual review of a Muslim-owned restaurant is not backbiting. Business reviews are a public service, not the exposure of a private sin.

title: Is obedience to parents unconditionally obligatory in Islam?
summary: Islam commands good conduct toward parents, not unconditional obedience. Obedience is obligatory only when the command is Islamically good, directly benefits the parents, and does not cause disproportionate hardship. You must never be rude regardless.

title: Am I obliged to remind a parent suffering from dementia to pray?
summary: When a person has dementia, the pen is lifted from them (no longer held accountable). Reminding them is good but not obligatory. In advanced stages, they lack the cognitive capacity required.

title: Is it permissible for Muslims to celebrate religious holidays of other faiths like Christmas or Diwali?
summary: Muslims do not celebrate religious festivals of other faiths. However, Muslim converts may attend family gatherings on Christmas to maintain family ties, as long as they avoid religious rituals.

title: Is it permissible for Muslims to celebrate Halloween?
summary: Halloween should be avoided due to lingering associations with demons and the supernatural, but it does not constitute shirk or kufr. Muslims who participate out of ignorance should be treated with empathy.

title: Is wearing the hijab obligatory in Islam and what is the wisdom behind it?
summary: The hijab (head covering and loose body covering) is obligatory for Muslim women by unanimous consensus of all schools of Islamic law—Sunni and beyond. The Quran explicitly commands this in Surah al-N
tags: can you take hijab off for safety
what does quran say about hijab

title: What constitutes impermissible imitation of non-Muslims (tashabbuh)?
summary: Prohibited imitation means deliberately adopting something that characterizes a civilization of kufr foreign to one's own. For Western Muslims, speaking English and wearing Western clothes is not imitation.

title: Is the mehndi (henna) ceremony before weddings permissible despite its Hindu origins?
summary: The mehndi ceremony is permissible despite its Hindu origins because the religious connotation has been completely lost. It is now a purely cultural practice among Muslims.

title: Does a practice having pagan origins make it haram for Muslims?
summary: Pagan origins alone do not make a practice haram. What matters is the current cultural association, not the historical origin. If pagan connotation is gone, the practice is permissible.

title: Are personal private celebrations like birthdays, graduations, and promotions permissible in Islam?
summary: Personal, private, non-religious celebrations are fully permissible. The Sharia is silent about them, and making something annual (like birthdays) does not make it bid'ah.

title: Is it permissible to have a secret marriage in Islam?
summary: A secret marriage may be technically valid if basic conditions are met (wali, witnesses, mahr), but it is strongly discouraged. No marriage will flourish if done in secret.

title: Is it permissible to participate in secular national holidays like the 4th of July?
summary: Secular communal celebrations like the 4th of July are cultural matters, not religious rituals. Individual participation through family gatherings, barbecues, and fireworks is permissible.

title: Is it permissible for Muslims to celebrate Thanksgiving?
summary: Thanksgiving is a secular, non-religious holiday with no ritual of deity worship. It is more neutral than Halloween and permissible for Muslims to participate in.

title: Is yoga permissible for Muslims?
summary: Yoga is permissible in the West where it is practiced purely as exercise. However, it should be avoided by Muslims in India where it retains strong Hindu religious associations.

title: If a husband and wife separate for years without a formal divorce, does the marriage still exist?
summary: Separation alone, even for years, does not constitute a divorce. If no talaq was uttered or filed, the marriage remains valid and can be resumed without a new nikah.

title: Is it permissible to short sell stocks in Islam?
summary: Short selling stocks is not permissible according to the vast majority of Islamic finance councils, primarily because you are selling what you do not own, which contradicts the Prophet's explicit hadith.

title: Can you shorten the prayer (qasr) when traveling for pleasure or vacation?
summary: By unanimous scholarly consensus, any permissible (mubah) travel qualifies for shortening prayer. Vacation and leisure trips are included — qasr is not limited to business travel.

title: Should the hijab be enforced by an Islamic government?
summary: Yasir Qadhi affirms that hijab is a clear religious obligation in Islam according to scholarly consensus, but argues that enforcing it through government coercion is counterproductive and not aligned

title: Can you combine the six fasts of Shawwal with making up missed Ramadan fasts?
summary: You cannot combine the six fasts of Shawwal with makeup Ramadan fasts under a dual intention. You may fast Mondays/Thursdays for makeup and get the sunnah reward. It is better to finish Ramadan fasts before Shawwal fasts, but not required.

title: Are student loans permissible in Islam?
summary: Student loans involving interest are riba and thus haram by default. However, the European Fiqh Council, AMJA, and the Fiqh Council of North America all permit students to take such loans when no alte
tags: is it haram to take student loans

title: Is there one correct 'Sunnah' way to pray salah?
summary: There is no single 'correct' way to pray that renders all other ways invalid. All four established Sunni schools of law (Hanafi, Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanbali) derive their prayer methods from authentic Su
tags: where to place hands in prayer
which madhab is correct for salah

tags: praying tarawih at home vs masjid
reading quran from phone in salah
can I hold mushaf in prayer

title: Does saying triple talaq in one sitting count as three divorces or one?
summary: The correct opinion, which Yasir Qadhi personally holds, is that a triple talaq given in one sitting constitutes only one talaq — not three. This was the position of Ibn Taymiyyah, who was famously ja
tags: does triple talaq count as one
saying talaq three times in anger
is triple talaq one or three

title: Is vanilla extract permissible in food despite containing alcohol?
summary: Vanilla extract in food is permissible. No food product flavored with vanilla can cause intoxication, so it does not fall under the prophetic prohibition. The same applies to nutmeg and similar extracts.

title: Can a woman travel for Hajj without a mahram?
summary: Yasir Qadhi holds that a woman may travel for Hajj (and other permissible or virtuous travel) without a mahram if the environment is generally safe, following the position of the Shafi'i school, Ibn T
tags: can a woman travel without mahram

title: Is it permissible for women to hold leadership roles and board positions in the masjid?
summary: Nothing in the Sharia forbids women from serving on a masjid board or holding leadership positions. Restricting women from permissible roles risks pushing them away from the community.

title: Is it haram to work for a company that has business ties to Israel?
summary: Yasir Qadhi differentiates between companies directly aiding and abetting genocide — such as those manufacturing weapons or building factories on occupied land — and generic corporations that merely d
tags: is my job haram if company does business with Israel

title: Is zakat due on a woman's gold jewelry?
summary: The safer position is that zakat is due on women's gold jewelry at 2.5%, even though the majority of scholars say it is not required on gold used for personal adornment. The Hanafi position, which requires zakat, has strong evidence since gold can function as currency.

title: How do you calculate zakat on long-term investments like 401k and mutual funds?
summary: For long-term investments not actively traded, zakat is due on one-third of the total value (2.5% of one-third). For day traders actively buying and selling, zakat is due on the full 100%.

title: Is cryptocurrency halal in Islam?
summary: Yasir Qadhi says major established cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin are generally permissible, with the condition that exchange-style transactions be done on the spot and with financial caution.

title: What acts are not permitted during intimacy between husband and wife?
summary: Only two acts are explicitly haram: anal intercourse (by unanimous consensus and explicit hadith) and vaginal intercourse during the menstrual period (by Quranic text and ijma'). All sexual positions are permissible. Other practices are neither commanded nor forbidden — what is culturally disliked is not automatically haram. Videotaping is strongly discouraged.
tags: what is haram in marriage bed

title: How do I make up years of salah that I missed?
summary: Two valid scholarly opinions exist. If able, make up the missed prayers (qada). If the number is too large to be feasibly made up, sincere repentance combined with increased istighfar, extra voluntary prayers, and charitable deeds is sufficient. Yasir Qadhi says: if you can make them up, do so; if not, follow the second opinion and repent sincerely.
tags: how to make up prayers islam
how many prayers to make up
is it obligatory to make up missed prayers

title: Can Muslims vote for a candidate who holds some un-Islamic views?
summary: Muslims should vote for the best available candidate based on key issues — primarily stopping genocide and protecting Muslim civil liberties — rather than expecting any politician to be a perfect Isla
tags: can Muslims vote for non-ideal candidate

title: How should a Muslim husband balance between his mother and his wife?
summary: A Muslim husband must simultaneously be a dutiful son and a protector of his wife — he cannot take sides. The Quran commands ihsan (kindness) to parents, not unconditional obedience, which is a critic
tags: how to balance mother and wife in Islam
obeying parents vs wife in Islam

title: Is it permissible to carry a small Quran in your wallet or car for protection and blessings?
summary: Carrying a physical Quran for baraka is not shirk and not haram — the Quran is the speech of Allah. There is a classical scholarly disagreement from the Companions on this. Yasir Qadhi personally leans toward active recitation being superior, but will not criticize those who keep a mushaf for protection.

title: Is Einstein's theory of relativity (E=mc²) incompatible with Islam?
summary: There is nothing wrong with Einstein's theory of relativity from an Islamic standpoint. Science and the Quran cannot genuinely conflict because both are from Allah. The only caveat: Allah can override the laws of physics — which is what a miracle is.

title: Can Muslims go to Western secular courts for marriage and divorce disputes?
summary: Going to Western courts is a case-by-case matter, not blanket haram. Muslims are encouraged to set up Islamic arbitration systems (like the Jewish bet din), which American law fully permits. A husband-initiated civil divorce generally counts as talaq; a wife-initiated one may count as khul'. Local scholars can ratify Islamic divorces on top of civil ones in cases of abandonment.

title: Is the term 'Sunni Islam' divisive? Why not just say 'Islam'?
summary: The term 'Sunni Islam' is not divisive in itself — it is descriptive. Different understandings of Islam have existed since the Companions. The term Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah is derived from authentic hadith and was first used by Ibn Abbas (RA). Misuse does not negate validity. The goal is to educate, not to declare others kafir.

title: Is it haram for a man to wear pants that go below the ankles (isbal)?
summary: The overwhelming majority of classical scholars hold that lowering garments below the ankle is only prohibited when done out of arrogance. In modern Western society, normal pants below the ankle carry no connotation of pride. This is the standard position of Abu Hanifa, Ibn Taymiyya, and all four madhabs.

title: Are all actions of the Prophet ﷺ binding upon Muslims, or only some?
summary: The Prophet's actions fall into three categories: religious rituals (fully binding), cultural habits of his time and place (not binding), and a gray area. His dress, cuisine, transportation, and architecture are not legislatively Sunnah. The real Sunnah of dress is to dress like your own people, which is what the Prophet himself did.

title: Did the Prophet ﷺ see Allah during Isra wal-Miraj?
summary: The majority of scholars hold that the Prophet (SAW) did not see Allah physically during Isra wal-Miraj. Aisha (RA) explicitly stated the Surah al-Najm passages refer to Jibreel. The Prophet did see Allah in a true dream (Tirmidhi/Ahmad). Believers will see Allah in Jannah — this is definitive Sunni doctrine.

title: What happens if you make an oath in Allah's name and cannot fulfill it? What is the kaffarah?
summary: If you make a sincere oath by Allah and cannot fulfill it, you must pay kaffarah: feed 10 poor people, clothe 10 poor people, or fast 3 days. If you see a better alternative, pursue it and still pay kaffarah. Intentional lying while swearing by Allah is a major sin requiring only sincere tawba.

title: Is Salat al-Shukr (prayer of gratitude) or Sajda al-Shukr a valid Sunnah?
summary: Sajda al-Shukr is definitively Sunnah — established by multiple authentic narrations. Salat al-Shukr (two rak'at) is at minimum permissible and not bid'ah, supported by hadith in Ibn Majah and the Prophet's practice at the conquest of Makkah. Salat al-Tawba is definitively Sunnah with a clear authentic hadith.
tags: is it bidah to pray shukr

title: What are the Islamic obligations for a child with special needs or severe autism?
summary: By unanimous scholarly consensus, religious obligations are lifted from those who lack rational capacity. Severely autistic individuals have no religious burden and will enter Jannah if raised in a Muslim home. Parents raising special-needs children earn immense reward. Obligations increase proportionally with cognitive capacity, which only parents can assess.

title: Can I pray Tarawih at home following a virtual imam via livestream?
summary: Praying behind a virtual or online imam does not constitute a valid jama'a. Physical proximity is required by all four madhabs and no major fiqh council has permitted online congregational prayer, even during COVID-19. However, praying your own independent salah while listening to a live broadcast for motivation is technically permissible.
tags: can I follow imam on TV

title: What are my Islamic duties toward parents who were abusive?
summary: The obligation of dutifulness to parents (birr al-walidayn) is diminished proportionally to the injustice they committed. A child suffering panic attacks from visiting an abusive parent is not required to do so. Some duties remain (e.g. financial help sent indirectly), but the obligation shrinks with the severity of the abuse.
tags: can I stop visiting abusive parents
do I have to serve abusive parents

title: Is it obligatory to take medicine or undergo medical treatment when sick?
summary: Taking medicine is not obligatory (wajib) according to any of the four major madhabs. The mainstream position is that medicine is mustahab (recommended) at most. It only becomes wajib in the narrow case where death is near-certain without treatment, the treatment is reliable, and it is accessible. Quality of life considerations are valid — a terminally ill person who refuses difficult treatment with uncertain odds is not sinning.

title: What are the Islamic etiquettes and rulings when a loved one passes away?
summary: Islam requires acceptance of Allah's qadar and sabr. Crying and grief are fully permitted — the Prophet (ﷺ) himself wept. Wailing (raising voice with phrases like "I can't live without you") is forbidden. The taziyah (offering condolences, visiting the bereaved) is Sunnah, as is gifting food to the grieving family. Good deeds can be performed on behalf of the deceased.
tags: sabr at death of loved one
is crying allowed when someone dies
inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi rajiun dua

title: What are the rulings of the widow's mourning period (haddad/iddah) in Islam?
summary: A widow must observe the haddad (mourning iddah) for four months and ten days — explicitly stated in Quran 2:234 and wajib. She must avoid decorative clothing, makeup, perfume, and jewelry while maintaining normal hygiene. The default is to remain in the marital home; she may leave for daytime needs. Genuine hardship allows staying with a mahram. No explicit marriage proposal is permitted during this period.
tags: can widow stay with family during iddah
what widow cannot do in iddah

title: Is it permissible to eat meat from Western supermarkets (Ahl al-Kitab meat)?
summary: Yasir Qadhi follows the majority position that western supermarket meat is not permissible: bismillah is not said, the slaughterer may not be a believing Christian or Jew given rising atheism, and stunning introduces uncertainty. Kosher meat IS halal. Those following the Shafi'i or Maliki positions may eat it — they have a valid basis. The "halal but not zabiha" distinction is cultural, not a fiqh category.

title: Can a woman recite the Quran during her menstrual cycle?
summary: The majority (Hanafi, Hanbali, Shafi'i) say no. The Maliki school and scholars like Ibn Taymiyya and Ibn Uthaymin permit it. If abstaining would significantly impact memorization or spirituality, following the permissive opinion privately is acceptable. Touching the physical mushaf is prohibited by all schools for anyone without wudu — use a screen, gloves, or cover instead.
tags: can women recite quran on period

title: Can a Muslim inherit from a non-Muslim, or leave inheritance to non-Muslim family?
summary: The hadith about Muslims and kafirs not inheriting from each other applies only to the Quran-mandated inheritance shares (fara'id), not to wills. Up to one-third of one's estate can always be given via will to anyone, including non-Muslims. A convert with no Muslim family in a Western country may direct their entire estate to non-Muslim relatives and may freely accept money left to them in a non-Muslim parent's will.

title: Is my job haram if my company has some haram dealings I'm not directly involved in?
stanceSummary: Whether your job becomes haram because your company has haram dealings is evaluated on five criteria: directness of involvement, intention, quantity of haram, industry-wide norm, and necessity. Incidental, unintentional, small-quantity haram that is not your direct responsibility does not make the entire job haram.

title: Why are governments banning the hijab, and what is the Islamic response?
stanceSummary: Hijab bans across Europe and India are not about the hijab itself — they are about the hijab as a visible symbol of Islamic civilization in a clash between religious identity and secular/colonialist worldviews. The pattern traces back to French colonization of Algeria. Muslims must obey the law while asserting their civic identity and not endorsing the morality of such bans.

title: Are misyar, secret nikah, and zawaj urfi permissible in Islam?
stanceSummary: Misyar (wife waives financial support/overnight stays) is technically halal per the majority of scholars but not encouraged and typically ends in divorce with the woman suffering more. Secret nikah (done with wali and witnesses but not publicized) is valid but makruh. Unregistered-but-public zawaj urfi is halal; boyfriend-girlfriend 'urfi' with no wali or witnesses is completely invalid.

title: How do you pray Salat al-Janazah correctly, and can women attend it?
stanceSummary: Salat al-Janazah has four takbirat with no ruku or sujud. Schools differ on reciting Fatiha (Hanbali/Shafi'i yes; Hanafi/Maliki no), number of tesleems (Shafi'i/Hanafi: two; Hanbali/Maliki: one), and additional dua after the 4th takbira. Women may pray janazah by unanimous consensus. Whether they may attend the burial is debated; Yasir Qadhi permits it if they maintain composure and do not wail.

title: What are the Islamic rulings on the corporate workplace — dress code, mixed interaction, and alcohol at events?
stanceSummary: Fiqh of corporate life must distinguish haram li dhatihi (forbidden in itself) from haram li ghayrihi (forbidden because it leads to haram). The second category may become makruh in a context of widespread unavoidability (balwa). Only the lustful gaze is haram — normal professional eye contact is at most makruh. A healthcare worker compelled to expose forearms is not committing major haram. Being present at a gathering with alcohol is not the same as drinking.

title: What is the difference between major and minor sins in Islam?
stanceSummary: A major sin (kabira) is one for which Allah prescribed a worldly hadd punishment, threatened jahannam, or cursed the perpetrator. The list is shorter than some scholars claim. Minor sins are forgiven by maintaining a basic religious lifestyle (prayers, fasting, istighfar). Major sins require specific tawbah. No major sin is unforgivable with sincere repentance, and a minor sin done arrogantly can be worse spiritually than a major sin done with remorse.

title: Do I have to pay zakat on my gold and silver jewelry?
stanceSummary: The majority of scholars (Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanbali) hold there is NO zakat on personal gold and silver jewelry, as personal-use items are exempt. The Hanafi school and modern Salafi scholars (Ibn Baz, Ibn Uthaymin) hold zakat IS due. Yasir Qadhi personally follows the Hanafi view for caution, even though the majority opinion has strong evidence. Follow your madhab, or if asked, the safer opinion is to give zakat.

title: Is there an authentic narration that the Prophet (ﷺ) contemplated suicide early in prophethood?
stanceSummary: A narration in Sahih Bukhari appears to describe the Prophet (ﷺ) going to mountaintops during the pause in revelation. However, the vast majority of hadith scholars say this portion is NOT from Aisha — it is from Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (two generations removed) saying "it has reached me," making it a broken chain. It is not authentic. A small minority of early scholars accepted it as a human fear at an early stage. The preacher who cited it was not disrespecting the Prophet — the narration exists in Bukhari — but likely did not know the hadith science behind it.

title: Is it permissible to make dua against someone who wronged you, and are you responsible for what happens to them?
tags: dua against someone who wronged you
am I responsible for what happens after my dua
stanceSummary: Making dua against someone who wronged you is completely permissible — it is one of the duas most guaranteed to be answered. You are not responsible for what happens to them afterward. Death is decreed by Allah; your dua cannot change someone's time of death. You should ask for what is proportionate to the wrong done. No kaffarah is required.

title: Can Muslims participate in rebuilding temples and churches after a disaster?
stanceSummary: Muslims are commanded to cooperate with non-Muslim neighbors in general humanitarian causes — rebuilding homes, providing food and shelter. However, actively participating in rebuilding a place of non-Muslim worship (temple, church) is a line Muslims should politely decline. This is analogous to a non-drinker refusing to help build a pub — having principled limits while remaining kind and cooperative in all other respects.

title: Is the Friday Jumu'ah khutbah required to be in Arabic?
tags: is juma khutba required in Arabic
stanceSummary: The majority of classical scholars said the khutbah should be in Arabic, but this is scholarly opinion — not an explicit command from Quran or Sunnah. All major global fatwa councils (Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Jordan, FCNA, European Fiqh Council) have ruled the khutbah may be in whatever language benefits the congregation most. Yasir Qadhi strongly advocates English (or local language) khutbahs in non-Arabic communities. The Prophet gave the khutbah in Arabic because it was his language — not because he mandated all future khutbahs must be in Arabic.

title: Is it permissible to conduct a nikah online or over Zoom?
summary: An online nikah over Zoom, Skype, or phone is permissible as long as identities can be confirmed through context. Physical presence in the same room is not required for a valid nikah.

title: Are the parents of the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) in Jannah?
summary: This is a genuine theological controversy with great scholars holding both opinions. Yasir Qadhi advises the average Muslim not to dwell on it, to follow whatever position their teacher taught, and to leave the judgment to Allah.

title: Should a Muslim woman change her last name to her husband's after marriage?
summary: A Muslim woman should not change her last name to her husband's — this is a European custom implying ownership, not an Islamic practice. The Quranic verse about calling people by their fathers refers to lineage, not surnames.
tags: woman last name after marriage islam

title: Is Wagyu beef halal?
summary: Wagyu beef is halal as long as it is properly slaughtered. The idea that Wagyu cows are fed beer is a myth — they are not. Even small amounts of impurity accidentally ingested by animals are overlooked by all scholars.

title: Does the Quran forbid Muslims from having non-Muslim friends?
summary: The Quran does not forbid Muslims from having non-Muslim friends. The verse about 'awliya' refers to community-level political/military alliances during conflict, not personal friendships. Allah explicitly permits the highest loyalty (birr) toward non-Muslims who are not hostile.
tags: do not take jews christians as friends
can muslims be friends with non-muslims

title: Is it permissible to be intimate with one's spouse after the nikah but before the walima?
summary: After the nikah, the couple is fully married in Allah's eyes and any intimacy is permissible. The walima is a celebration, not a condition of marriage. However, there are legal consequences (mahr and iddah) if the couple was alone and a divorce later occurs — so open communication with families is advised.
tags: can husband see wife before walima

title: Can a Muslim inherit from a non-Muslim family member?
summary: Muslims can inherit from non-Muslim relatives and non-Muslims can receive bequests from Muslims. The hadith about mutual non-inheritance applies to the fixed Quranic shares (faraid), not to gifts or wills. In the West, converts with no Muslim heirs may freely distribute their estate as they wish.

title: Does the Quran's mention of 'white' and 'black' faces on Judgment Day have racial meaning?
summary: The Quranic references to 'white' and 'black' faces on Judgment Day are Arabic idioms meaning radiant with joy vs. darkened with shame — they have no racial meaning whatsoever. Any translator who renders them as skin color has misunderstood or mistranslated the Arabic.
tags: surah al imran white black faces

title: Is Salat al-Tasbih (the Prayer of Glorification) authentically established in the Sunnah?
summary: Salat al-Tasbih has highly contested authenticity — early hadith masters considered it weak while later scholars authenticated it. Yasir Qadhi personally doesn't pray it but says it is absolutely not a bid'ah. Those who pray it have valid scholarly precedent.

title: Can a Muslim woman get married without her father's permission?
summary: A wali is required for nikah per the majority position. If the wali is genuinely unreasonable, a senior imam can facilitate the marriage. But 'unreasonable' must be assessed by a neutral third party — not by the bride herself, whose emotions are too involved.
tags: can woman marry without father islam

title: Does the hadith "la adwa" mean Islam denies that diseases are contagious?
summary: The hadith 'la adwa' negates the pagan superstition that sickness transfers supernaturally from proximity alone — it does not deny natural disease transmission. Multiple other sahih hadith prove the Prophet ﷺ fully accepted physical contagion.

title: Is it obligatory (wajib) to take medicine or undergo medical treatment in Islam?
summary: Taking medicine is mustahab (recommended), not wajib (obligatory), per the majority of all four schools. It becomes obligatory only if death is nearly certain without an easily accessible treatment. Declining treatment is not a sin in most circumstances.
tags: is it haram to refuse treatment

title: What does Islam say about the obligations of a child toward abusive parents?
summary: Parental abuse reduces but does not eliminate a child's Islamic obligations. Severe dhulm from parents proportionally reduces the child's obligations. The Sharia does not impose what one cannot bear — if visiting causes genuine psychological harm, it is not required. Pursue reconciliation through family but don't give up.

title: Can you pray Tarawih at home following a live-streamed imam online?
summary: Online jamaa prayer is not permissible per all major fiqh councils. Physical proximity is a condition of jama. A rare concession exists: one may pray an independent prayer while listening to a live broadcast recitation — not as a follower but as a synchronized independent worshipper.

title: Is Jumuah obligatory when masjids re-open with COVID precautions? Are social-distancing gaps in prayer rows valid?
summary: Jumu'ah is obligatory again for healthy people once mosques re-open with precautions; those with health conditions retain an excuse. Social-distancing gaps in prayer rows are permissible — continuous rows are Sunnah not wajib, and necessity makes them even more acceptable during a plague.

title: Can a woman recite or memorize the Quran while she is menstruating?
summary: Two valid scholarly positions exist. The majority forbids Quran recitation during menses. The Maliki school and Ibn Taymiyyah permit it, saying no authentic hadith explicitly forbids it. A woman may follow the minority opinion if her memorization would be harmed. Touching the mushaf physically is prohibited by all schools regardless.
tags: can woman read quran on period

title: My spouse had a haram relationship before we married. Is the nikah valid? What should I do?
summary: The nikah is fully valid regardless of pre-marital sins. Disclosure of past repented sins is not required. The deceived spouse cannot be forced to stay but should seriously consider forgiving — the discovery can actually strengthen the marriage if the spouse has genuinely repented.
tags: should i tell spouse about past sin
is nikah valid if sin before marriage

title: What types of animal leather are halal to use in Islam? Does tanning purify hides?
summary: Tanning purifies animal skin through istihala (chemical transformation). All leather from edible animals — whether or not slaughtered — is permissible after tanning. Pig leather remains prohibited per Yasir Qadhi's fatwa (Hanafi position), though some scholars allow even that.

title: If Hajj is cancelled, can I use my saved Hajj money for other expenses?
summary: Using Hajj savings is completely permissible when Hajj is cancelled. The money is yours. Your intention to perform Hajj is rewarded fully by Allah regardless. Try to save again for next year if possible.

title: What is the Islamic ruling on oral intercourse between husband and wife?
summary: Oral intercourse between spouses is not haram — there is no authentic prohibition. Some classical scholars explicitly permitted it. It is considered makruh (disliked) due to the risk of najasa ingestion. A wife is not obligated to perform it as it is beyond standard marital intimacy.
tags: is oral sex haram in islam

title: Why do Muslims not celebrate Pride Month? What is the Islamic position on same-sex relationships?
summary: Same-sex actions are haram by unanimous 14-century scholarly consensus. The Quran's story of Lut addresses this topic explicitly and repeatedly. Muslims cannot celebrate Pride Month but must distinguish between hating the act and hating the person — cooperation for generic good is permitted, but facilitating or normalizing what the Sharia prohibits is not.

title: What are the Islamic rules of mourning and the widow's iddah (ihdad)?
summary: A widow's iddah is four months and ten days (ihdad). She should avoid decorative clothing/makeup/jewelry, remain in her marital home as the default (leaving during the day for needs is permitted), and cannot receive or give an explicit marriage proposal during this period.
tags: iddah widow four months ten days
what to do when someone dies islam

title: Why do Muslims debate who is in or out of Islam? How should we deal with sectarianism?
summary: Defining what is and isn't correct Islamic belief is necessary. But judging an action as wrong is different from condemning the person. Non-mainstream Muslims who affirm the core tenets are within the fold of Islam even if their beliefs are problematic. We correct incorrect theology with wisdom while treating people with dignity.
tags: how to deal with different muslim sects

title: Does the Quran misunderstand the Christian Trinity? Is the Quran's description of Christian beliefs incorrect?
summary: The Quran addresses the lived beliefs of Arab Christians of the 7th century, not abstract church councils. Academic research confirms Arab Christians of that era held near-tritheistic views. The Quran's terminology is therefore precise, not mistaken.
tags: does quran say mary is god

title: Is Zakat due on 401(k) and IRA retirement funds? How is it calculated?
summary: Zakat is due annually on 401(k)/IRA retirement funds per the Fiqh Council of North America. It is calculated on the amount accessible after taxes and penalties — not the full paper balance. If you cannot pay in full, write down the debt to Allah and pay when you cash out.
tags: how to calculate zakat on retirement

title: Is it permissible to eat non-halal meat from supermarkets? What is the ruling on Ahl al-Kitab meat?
summary: Non-halal supermarket meat is not permissible per the majority position. The Quranic permission for Ahl al-Kitab meat applies only when they follow their own sharia of slaughter. Modern commercial meat fails on three counts: basmala not mentioned, workers may not be believers, and stunning may kill before slaughter. Kosher meat is an exception and is halal.

title: What are the Islamic rulings on COVID-19 medical decisions: intubation, ventilators, and end-of-life care?
summary: A Muslim is not obligated to accept ventilation/intubation for COVID-19 if the outcome is uncertain and they find it undignified. Every adult should prepare an advance directive. Withdrawing life support when there is no realistic benefit is permissible. These decisions must be made by the patient or their proxy, not physicians alone.

title: Should Muslims follow local moon sighting, a global sighting, or astronomical calculations for Ramadan?
summary: Yasir Qadhi personally prefers local moon sighting but endorses astronomical calculations for Western Muslims (the Fiqh Council of North America's position). Communal unity is a recognized Islamic value, and the practical benefits of predictable dates for Western Muslim institutions are decisive.
tags: can muslims use calculations for ramadan

title: Does one have to make up Witr prayer if it was missed?
summary: Hanafis must make up Witr since it is wajib in their school. For Malikis, Shafi'is, and Hanbalis, Witr is a strong sunnah but not obligatory, so no makeup is required though an optional compensatory prayer is recommended.

title: How does one perform the Sajda of Tilawa (prostration of recitation)?
summary: Sajda Tilawa is a recommended sunnah (not obligatory) performed by saying takbir to go down into sajda and takbir to come back up. Wudu and facing the qibla are required per the majority position. There is no opening takbir and no tasleem.
tags: how many sajda verses in quran

title: Can a Muslim enter Makkah without wearing Ihram?
summary: Entering Makkah without Ihram is permissible when one is not performing Umrah or Hajj. If one later intends to perform Umrah, they must go to the miqat and enter Ihram from there. There is also a concession to declare Ihram verbally and wear regular clothes due to a legitimate need, incurring a smaller penalty.
tags: is ihram required to enter makkah
can doctor enter makkah without ihram

title: Is it permissible to give a baby milk from a milk bank?
summary: Permissible according to the position Yasir Qadhi follows (al-Qaradawi and al-Ashqar), because hundreds of women's milk is mixed, making each individual donor's contribution far below the threshold (5 full feedings) required to establish a foster relationship. A genuine need is preferred before resorting to a milk bank.

title: How does one determine if a hadith is authentic?
summary: Laypeople cannot verify hadith and must rely on scholars of hadith just as a patient relies on a doctor. Hadith in Bukhari and Muslim are generally authentic. Weak hadith may be acted upon for voluntary worship but not for establishing rulings of halal/haram or creed.

title: Is a 401K early withdrawal repayment penalty considered haram interest?
summary: A 401K repayment penalty is not riba (interest) because the money goes from your pocket back into your own account — no third party is involved. Riba requires a payment flowing from one party to another.

title: Can one pay Zakat on behalf of someone else?
summary: Zakat is the obligation of the wealth's owner. A husband may voluntarily pay his wife's Zakat as a gift but is not required to. Guardians of orphans should pay Zakat from the orphan's own wealth, not their personal funds. Paying Zakat on behalf of another is permissible if done voluntarily.
tags: zakat on behalf of someone else
can someone else give your zakat

title: Does the Quran or Sunnah explain the phenomenon of Déjà Vu?
summary: Islam has no explicit text addressing Déjà Vu, but the Islamic concept of the soul offers possible indirect explanations: souls may have interacted before birth in a pre-earthly existence, and the soul's nightly departure during sleep may embed subconscious memories. These are theories, not confirmed teachings.
tags: ibn hazm souls recognize each other
why do i feel ive met someone before islam

title: Can a Muslim ask Allah for a personal miracle?
summary: Asking Allah for what appears miraculous because you genuinely need it is the essence of iman. Asking for a miracle to prove Allah's existence or power carries an element of arrogance and is problematic — the miracles of existence itself and the Quran are already sufficient.
tags: can i ask allah for miracle
is it arrogance to ask allah for sign
is it allowed to ask for miracle in islam

title: How do we understand the hadith that says the gender of a baby is determined by which fluid overpowers the other?
summary: The apparent conflict dissolves when you compare the Bukhari narration (Anas ibn Malik) against the Muslim narration (Tawban): the question was about child resemblance, not gender. The word 'sabaka' (overpowers/dominates) maps onto the modern genetics concept of dominant genes/alleles. Ibn Taymiyyah reached the same conclusion 800 years ago.
tags: hadith man fluid woman fluid gender

title: How should a Muslim community handle differences of opinion and practices within a single Masjid?
summary: When the Muslim world's mainstream scholars have disagreed about a practice, a small local community cannot enforce one view on the other. Live and let live: allow both sides to state their positions respectfully, permit the practices that have scholarly precedent, and never split the community over issues that remain contested across the Muslim world.
tags: can we split masjid over fiqh

title: Can Jumu'ah prayer be prayed before the sun reaches its zenith (zawal)?
summary: The Hanbali school permits Jumu'ah up to an hour before zawal. The other three schools require it to be after zawal. When a genuine need exists (e.g., students can only pray during a fixed early lunch break), following the Hanbali position to preserve the Jumu'ah is permissible.

title: What does the hadith about dying without an oath of allegiance (bay'a) mean today?
summary: The bay'a hadith applies when a legitimate caliphate exists. Today, with no caliphate, the hadith is not applicable — 'fear Allah as much as you can.' The Quraysh condition was historically contingent and no longer operative according to many scholars. Avoid fringe movements that use this hadith to demand exclusive allegiance.
tags: dying without bay'ah death of jahiliyyah
no caliphate do we need bay'a

title: What should a Muslim do when they have doubts about specific hadiths that conflict with modern science?
summary: Doubting or rejecting specific hadiths based on sound scholarly methodology is within mainstream Sunni Islam and is not kufr. The certainties of faith are not threatened by questions about individual narrations. If a hadith seems problematic, first check if scholars disputed its authenticity, then check if your understanding is correct, then resort to tafwid (resigning its meaning to Allah). Never use minor data points to challenge major certainties.
tags: how to deal with hadith doubts
is it kufr to reject hadith

title: Can a brother act as wali for his sister's marriage if their father unjustly refuses?
summary: An unjust wali who refuses a marriage for petty reasons (like ethnicity) can have his wilaya transferred by a qualified Islamic authority (a respected scholar in the West). But the first step is always to work with the father through family pressure and community channels. Never bypass the father without exhausting all options and involving a qualified third party.

title: How should a Muslim respond to the LGBTQ+ movement in a respectful but principled way?
summary: Islam clearly prohibits same-sex relationships and gender transition. However, Muslims must communicate this with prophetic wisdom — not by acquiescing under social pressure, nor by speaking with vulgarity. Same-sex attraction itself is not a sin; acting on it is. Muslims can and should remain kind and civil with LGBTQ individuals in daily life.
tags: is being gay haram in islam
how to deal with lgbtq classmates as muslim
what does islam say about gay people

title: Are secret marriages (nikah) permissible in Islam?
summary: A secret nikah is technically valid if conditions are met (wali, two witnesses, mahr) but is strongly discouraged (makruh) and contrary to the spirit of Islam. Such marriages will not flourish. Men should marry properly and not ask witnesses to hide the marriage.
tags: is nikah valid without telling anyone
does nikah have to be announced

title: Does filing for civil divorce in a non-Muslim country count as Islamic talaq?
summary: Husband filing for civil divorce = valid Islamic talaq. Wife filing for civil divorce does not automatically count as talaq, but with a scholar's validation of legitimate grounds (abuse, abandonment), the Fiqh Council of North America considers it a valid khul'.
tags: does civil divorce count in islam
is civil divorce valid in islam
husband files divorce counts as talaq

title: Can you pray salah in your own language?
summary: The arkan and wajibat of salah must be done in Arabic. Dua outside of salah can be made in any language by unanimous consensus. Dua inside salah (e.g., personal supplications in sujood) — Yasir Qadhi strongly endorses the opinion that it is permissible in any language.

title: Are a convert's good deeds from before Islam rewarded?
summary: Yes. Based on the hadith of Hakim ibn Hizzam, the Prophet (SAW) said: "You have accepted Islam along with all of the good deeds that you have done." A convert receives a double blessing: all prior sins are wiped clean, and good deeds done before Islam are preserved as reward.
tags: are good deeds before Islam counted

title: Does Allah love us unconditionally? Should Muslims say "God loves us"?
summary: Allah is Al-Wadud (the Ever-Loving), but saying "God loves us unconditionally" is not the same as affirming Allah is loving by nature. The Quran challenges this claim. Allah specifies eight categories of people He loves and roughly a dozen He does not. The correct approach is to say "I want Allah's love" and "I hope Allah loves me" — categorically claiming "Allah loves me" is a sign of arrogance.

title: Should you deduct mortgage debt from your savings when calculating zakat?
summary: No. Long-term debt like a mortgage is not deducted from zakatable wealth. If you allowed deducting the full remaining mortgage, most homeowners would never pay zakat for 30 years — this goes against the goals of the Sharia. Pay zakat on savings held for a year regardless of outstanding long-term debts.
tags: do I deduct mortgage from zakat

title: Is family planning and contraception permissible in Islam?
summary: Permissible, with one exception: it is sinful if the reason is believing you cannot financially afford children, which contradicts the Quranic promise that Allah provides. Any contraceptive that does not permanently harm the body is allowed. The practice is ja'iz (permitted) but makruh (discouraged).

title: How can a Muslim enter Jannatul Firdous (the highest level of Paradise)?
summary: Allah lists seven qualities of those who inherit Al-Firdous in Surah Al-Mu'minun: humility in prayer, avoiding useless speech, giving zakat, guarding private parts, faithfulness to trusts and pledges, and protecting prayers. Additionally, loving Allah and His Messenger is a path, and making constant sincere dua asking specifically for Al-Firdous.
tags: how to get to heaven Islam

title: Does the unethical treatment of animals make their meat haram?
summary: No. Halal status is determined by the animal being permissible to eat and being slaughtered properly. However, the farmer/livestock owner who mistreats animals is sinful before Allah. The consumer has the right to choose humane farms and to lobby for legislation, but cannot declare the meat haram for others.

title: Can a Muslim who experiences same-sex attraction marry someone of the opposite sex?
summary: A Muslim with exclusive same-sex attraction must not enter a marriage without fully disclosing this to the prospective spouse. Doing so is a major injustice and is sinful. The brother is not sinful for his inclinations, but is sinful for obeying parents in a way that harms an innocent third party.
tags: can a gay Muslim get married

title: Is homeopathy halal in Islam?
summary: From an Islamic legal perspective, homeopathy is permissible as long as it is believed to work through physical cause-and-effect (not supernatural means) and any alcohol is present only as a solvent in non-intoxicating quantities. Yasir Qadhi personally believes it is medically ineffective, but his personal opinion does not affect the ruling.

title: Is sarcasm halal or haram in Islam?
summary: Sarcasm among friends where everyone is laughing together and no one is targeted or hurt is permissible. Sarcasm meant to denigrate, belittle, or hurt someone's feelings is not permissible. The key distinction: laughing with people is allowed; laughing at people is not.

title: Is it possible to fight an effective war while adhering to Islamic ethics? What does Islam say about weapons of mass destruction?
summary: Islamic war ethics operates on two levels: theory (the ideal — no civilian casualties, no WMDs) and reality (reciprocity when the enemy violates the ideal). The Quranic principle of qisas: if they transgress against you, you may respond in kind. A purely theoretical approach would have led to the annihilation of Muslims throughout history.

title: Is a non-Muslim couple's marriage considered valid in Islam? Do they need to redo their nikah after converting?
summary: A non-Muslim couple's marriage is valid in Islam if they undertook a formal ceremony recognized by their own culture and the parties are permitted to marry each other. Cohabitation alone is not a marriage. If the couple converts to Islam, they do not need to redo their nikah. All four madhabs agree on this.

title: Are Muslim community festivals and suhoor fests permissible in Islam?
summary: Not categorically haram. Declaring a large Muslim public gathering haram simply because some may act improperly is an overreach. Whether attending is advisable depends on your level of piety and intention. For the highly religious, spending Ramadan in worship is better. For those at lower levels of practice, it may actually serve as an iman boost. Actions are judged by intentions.

title: Do Muslims need scholars or clergy to connect with Allah? Is depending on scholars like the Catholic Church?
summary: Muslims do not need scholars to connect with Allah, make dua, or develop spirituality — Allah hears directly with no intermediary needed. However, understanding Islamic law and theology does require trained scholarship. These are two completely separate needs. Raw spirituality without formal study is entirely valid.
tags: do I need imam for dua

title: Is it heresy to question the hadith about Aisha's age at marriage? Can Muslims reject the narration saying she was 9?
summary: Questioning the narration that Aisha was 9 at consummation is NOT heresy — it is a point of history, not theology. Both positions (accepting or questioning) have valid scholarly arguments within mainstream Islam. The narrator Hisham ibn Urwah never met Aisha and some hadith scholars note a mathematical discrepancy.
tags: is rejecting age of Aisha kufr

title: Is it haram to talk to the opposite gender in Islam? What are the limits of male-female interaction?
summary: Talking to the opposite gender is not haram in itself — no scholar has ever said so. What IS explicitly haram by hadith is khalwa (private seclusion alone with a non-mahram). The principle of sadd al-dhara'i is valid but culturally sensitive. Context, intention, and content all matter.
tags: can Muslim men talk to women

title: How do you distinguish between jinn possession and mental illness? When should you seek ruqyah vs. a doctor?
summary: As a default, 90–99% of cases believed to involve jinn are actually natural mental/psychological issues. Common disorders like schizophrenia and depression have natural causes. Some cases are genuinely jinn-related but this is a minority. Ruqyah on yourself is always safe. Only if you notice strange reactions to Quran recitation should you consult an experienced practitioner.
tags: how to know if you have jinn

title: Is it ethical for Muslim charities to pay high overhead costs and influencer fees? What percentage is acceptable?
summary: Some overhead is inevitable and Islamically legitimate — the Quran allocates zakat to amils. 15–20% overhead is acceptable; 50–60% is not. Percentage-based fees for fundraisers are absolutely unethical in Yasir Qadhi's view. His personal condition: always a flat fee from organizers' funds, never from donations raised.
tags: how much charity overhead is acceptable
